text:
Once upon a time, in the cozy town of Marshmallow, there lived a peculiar old owl named Olga. Olga had the curious habit of always wearing a big, floppy hat, even indoors. Every morning she would announce her intentions with a squawk, "Today I shall meditate on the significance of raisins in oatmeal!"

Every year on the full moon, Olga gathered all the owlets of Marshmallow for her big owl moon meditation. It wasn’t just any meditation; it was an owl council meeting. Each owl had a life story to tell—some had learned the virtues of raisins, others had adventures with curious cats, and a few were just obsessed with silent film stars.

One particular moon was especially tranquil. The owlets hooted softly as Olga recounted her time flying over the rainforest, discovering a family of endangered monkeys who loved peanut butter. Their friendship transformed her into a gentle creature who rarely flew away again.
